mission.title: kong: [keyauth] Add auto expiration to keys
mission.description: [keyauth] Add auto expiration to keys. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current kong deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kong version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
helm list -n kong 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kong installation.

steps.title: Review kong configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kong configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl get configmap -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kong
```
For example, to auto-expire in `2d` or `1y` since the creation date.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [keyauth] Add auto expiration to keys
steps.description:
I am glad to close this PR now on behalf of  who fixed it by submitting a PR #4984. It took some four and half years to get this in, but we finally did, ;-). Perhaps it was worth the wait, like all the good stuff. It feels better if it just doesn't come given. Haha... well thanks again ! Closing this now.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl get events -n kong --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[keyauth] Add auto expiration to keys" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I am glad to close this PR now on behalf of  who fixed it by submitting a PR #4984. It took some four and half years to get this in, but we finally did, ;-). Perhaps it was worth the wait, like all the good stuff. It feels better if it just doesn't come given. Haha... well thanks again ! Closing this now.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kong installed or the issue environment reproducible.
